Y.description: The quantity of paper rolls consumed every hour by a machine that operates on cash.

U.description: The lowest amount of individuals that need to be handled per hour.

A.description: The number of individuals processed by a cash-operated machine within an hour.

V.description: The highest quantity of paper rolls that can be utilized within a single hour

K.description: The number of individuals a card-only machine can handle in one hour.

W.description: Quantity of paper rolls utilized hourly by a machine that only accepts cards

assumptions.description: The quantity of paper rolls consumed every hour by a machine that operates on cash must be non-negative.
The lowest amount of individuals that need to be handled per hour must be non-negative.
The number of individuals processed by a cash-operated machine within an hour must be non-negative.
The highest quantity of paper rolls that can be utilized within a single hour must be non-negative.
The number of individuals a card-only machine can handle in one hour must be non-negative.
The quantity of paper rolls utilized hourly by a machine that only accepts cards must be non-negative.

r.description: The quantity of machines that exclusively accept cards

s.description: The quantity of machines that operate using cash.

slack_0.description: Slack variable for constraint: The minimum number of individuals handled within an hour by machines accepting both cash and cards must be U.

slack_1.description: Slack variable for constraint: The quantity of machines that only accept cards should not surpass the quantity of machines that accept cash.

slack_2.description: Slack variable for constraint: The combined amount of paper rolls used per hour by machines that accept cash and those that only accept cards should not surpass V.

constraints.description: The minimum number of individuals handled within an hour by machines accepting both cash and cards must be U. (Modified to include slack variable slack_0)
constraints.formulation: A * s + K * r - slack_0 = U

code.gurobipy: model.addConstr(A * s + K * r - slack_0 == U)

constraints.description: The quantity of machines that only accept cards should not surpass the quantity of machines that accept cash. (Modified to include slack variable slack_1)
The combined amount of paper rolls used per hour by machines that accept cash and those that only accept cards should not surpass V. (Modified to include slack variable slack_2)
constraints.formulation: s * Y + r * W + slack_2 = V

code.gurobipy: model.addConstr(s * Y + r * W + slack_2 == V)

constraints.description: The number of cash machines must be non-negative.
The number of card machines must be non-negative.
Slack variable slack_0 must be non-negative.
Slack variable slack_1 must be non-negative.
Slack variable slack_2 must be non-negative.

objective.description: Reduce the overall quantity of machines at the park.

notes: Introduce slack variables to convert inequality constraints into equality constraints.
